key_insight: Morphology engineering enabled the first robust lateral electrical and magnetotransport measurements in chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)2PbI4, revealing p-type in-plane conduction.
engineering_relevance: Controlling thin-film grain structure can unlock device integration and spintronic characterization of otherwise highly resistive chiral perovskites.
method: Optimized thin-film morphology for lateral device fabrication
Built Hall-bar devices for dark Hall and magnetotransport measurements
Compared transport paths crossing versus avoiding grain boundaries
evidence_signal: Hall data under dark conditions showed p-type conduction with ~0.2 cm^2 V^-1 s^-1 mobility and ~3×10^14 cm^-2 carrier density, and magnetoresistance increased along paths crossing grain boundaries.
watch_item: In-plane transport remains strongly morphology-dependent, so grain boundaries and film uniformity may limit reproducibility and device performance.
paper_title: Morphology control and low-temperature magnetotransport in chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)$_2$PbI$_4$
